enonce.texte:
Read the following text about preparing for a job interview and answer the questions below.

Preparing for a Job Interview

When you are invited for a job interview, you need to prepare carefully. First, research the company and the position you are applying for. Then, practice answering common interview questions. It is also important to dress appropriately and arrive on time. During the interview, make eye contact, listen carefully, and answer questions clearly. After the interview, you can send a thank-you note to show your appreciation. Good preparation increases your chances of getting the job.

questions.texte: What are the key steps to prepare for a job interview according to the text?
questions.reponse: The key steps are: research the company and position, practice answering interview questions, dress appropriately, arrive on time, make eye contact, listen carefully, answer questions clearly, and send a thank-you note.

questions.texte: Why is it important to research the company before an interview?
questions.reponse: Researching the company helps you understand what they do, their values, and what they expect from candidates. This allows you to tailor your answers and show that you are genuinely interested in the position and the organization.

questions.texte: What is the purpose of sending a thank-you note after the interview?
questions.reponse: Sending a thank-you note shows appreciation, professionalism, and good manners. It also helps you stand out from other candidates and reinforces your interest in the position.

enonce.texte: Transform the following sentences from active voice to passive voice and vice versa.

questions.texte: The company invited him for an interview. (Active to Passive)
questions.reponse: He was invited for an interview by the company.

questions.texte: The candidate answered all the questions correctly. (Active to Passive)
questions.reponse: All the questions were answered correctly by the candidate.

questions.texte: The job application was submitted by the applicant. (Passive to Active)
questions.reponse: The applicant submitted the job application.

questions.texte: The manager will give the results next week. (Active to Passive)
questions.reponse: The results will be given by the manager next week.

questions.texte: The training program was organized by the human resources department. (Passive to Active)
questions.reponse: The human resources department organized the training program.

enonce.texte:
Read the following dialogue between an interviewer (Mr. Nkolo) and a candidate (Sarah) during a job interview. Then answer the questions below.

Job Interview Dialogue

Mr. Nkolo: Good morning, Sarah. Please, have a seat.

Sarah: Good morning, Mr. Nkolo. Thank you for having me.

Mr. Nkolo: So, tell me about yourself and why you want to work for our company.

Sarah: I am a graduate in Business Administration and I am passionate about marketing. I have always admired your company's innovative approach to customer service, and I would love to contribute to your team.

Mr. Nkolo: That's interesting. What are your main strengths?

Sarah: I am very organized, I work well under pressure, and I am a good team player. I also have excellent communication skills.

Mr. Nkolo: Can you tell me about a challenge you faced in your previous job and how you handled it?

Sarah: Certainly. In my previous job, I had to manage a project with a tight deadline. I organized a meeting with my team, delegated tasks, and we managed to complete the project on time.

Mr. Nkolo: Very impressive. Do you have any questions for us?

Sarah: Yes, I would like to know more about the opportunities for professional development within the company.

questions.texte: Why did Sarah want to work for the company?
questions.reponse: She admired the company's innovative approach to customer service.

questions.texte: List two strengths that Sarah mentioned during the interview.
questions.reponse: Sarah mentioned that she is very organized and works well under pressure.

questions.texte: How did Sarah handle the challenge with the tight deadline in her previous job?
questions.reponse: She organized a meeting with her team, delegated tasks, and they managed to complete the project on time.

questions.texte: What question did Sarah ask at the end of the interview? Why is this a good question?
questions.reponse: Sarah asked about opportunities for professional development within the company. This is a good question because it shows that she is interested in growing within the company and is thinking about her long-term career.

enonce.texte: You are preparing for your first job interview. Fill out the following information form to help you prepare effectively.

questions.texte: Fill out the job interview preparation form with your own information.
questions.reponse:
JOB INTERVIEW PREPARATION FORM

1. Personal Information:
- Name: [Your Name]
- Phone Number: [Your Number]
- Email: [Your Email]

2. Position Applied For:
[Position Title]

3. Why I am interested in this position:
[Describe why you want the job]

4. My Strengths:
- [Strength 1]
- [Strength 2]
- [Strength 3]

5. My Weaknesses (and how I overcome them):
- [Weakness 1] - [Strategy to improve]
- [Weakness 2] - [Strategy to improve]

6. Questions to ask the interviewer:
- Question 1
- Question 2
- Question 3

7. What to wear:
[Describe appropriate outfit]

8. Documents to bring:
- CV/Resume
- Cover Letter
- References
- Certificates/Diplomas

enonce.texte:
Write a short guide (about 100-120 words) for young people preparing for their first job interview. Include the following elements:

  • Three important steps to prepare.
  • Advice on how to behave during the interview.
  • Use at least two examples of the passive voice (e.g., "Questions should be answered clearly.").

questions.texte: Write the guide for young people preparing for their first job interview.
questions.reponse:
YOUR FIRST JOB INTERVIEW: A GUIDE

Preparing for your first job interview can be stressful, but with good preparation, you can succeed. Here are three important steps:

1. Research the company: Find out what the company does and what they value. This information should be used to tailor your answers.

2. Practice common questions: Prepare answers about your strengths, weaknesses, and experiences. These should be practiced with a friend or in front of a mirror.

3. Plan your outfit: Choose professional clothing. Make sure it is clean and appropriate for the work environment.

During the interview, arrive on time, make eye contact, listen carefully, and answer questions confidently. Remember to be polite and show enthusiasm for the position. After the interview, send a thank-you note to the interviewer.

With these tips, you will be well-prepared to impress your potential employer. Good luck!
